metadata.description: A collection of agent skills that extend capabilities across planning, development, and tooling.

plugins.name: to-prd
plugins.description: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and submit it as a GitHub issue. No interview - just synthesizes what you've already discussed.

plugins.name: to-issues
plugins.description: Break any plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable GitHub issues using vertical slices.

plugins.name: grill-me
plugins.description: Get relentlessly interviewed about a plan or design until every branch of the decision tree is resolved.

plugins.name: design-an-interface
plugins.description: Generate multiple radically different interface designs for a module using parallel sub-agents.

plugins.name: request-refactor-plan
plugins.description: Create a detailed refactor plan with tiny commits via user interview, then file it as a GitHub issue.

plugins.name: domain-model
plugins.description: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise.

plugins.name: ubiquitous-language
plugins.description: Extract a DDD-style ubiquitous language glossary from the current conversation.

plugins.name: zoom-out
plugins.description: Tell the agent to zoom out and give broader context or a higher-level perspective. Use when you're unfamiliar with a section of code or need to understand how it fits into the bigger picture.

plugins.name: tdd
plugins.description: Test-driven development with a red-green-refactor loop. Builds features or fixes bugs one vertical slice at a time.

plugins.name: triage-issue
plugins.description: Investigate a bug by exploring the codebase, identify the root cause, and file a GitHub issue with a TDD-based fix plan.

plugins.name: github-triage
plugins.description: Triage GitHub issues through a label-based state machine with interactive grilling sessions. Use when reviewing incoming bugs or feature requests, or preparing issues for an AFK agent.

plugins.name: qa
plugins.description: Interactive QA session where user reports bugs or issues conversationally, and the agent files GitHub issues. Explores the codebase in the background for context and domain language.

plugins.name: improve-codebase-architecture
plugins.description: Explore a codebase for architectural improvement opportunities, focusing on deepening shallow modules and improving testability.

plugins.name: migrate-to-shoehorn
plugins.description: Migrate test files from `as` type assertions to @total-typescript/shoehorn.

plugins.name: scaffold-exercises
plugins.description: Create exercise directory structures with sections, problems, solutions, and explainers.

plugins.name: setup-pre-commit
plugins.description: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged, Prettier, type checking, and tests.

plugins.name: git-guardrails-claude-code
plugins.description: Set up Claude Code hooks to block dangerous git commands (push, reset --hard, clean, etc.) before they execute.

plugins.name: write-a-skill
plugins.description: Create new skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources.

plugins.name: edit-article
plugins.description: Edit and improve articles by restructuring sections, improving clarity, and tightening prose.

plugins.name: obsidian-vault
plugins.description: Search, create, and manage notes in an Obsidian vault with wikilinks and index notes.

plugins.name: caveman
plugins.description: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy.
